description: Read tweets from the home timeline on Twitter/X. Extracts tweet text, author handle, timestamp, engagement counts (likes, retweets, replies), and media presence. Supports filtering by content type.
author: stillwater

reasoning.research: Twitter/X home timeline renders tweets as article[data-testid='tweet'] elements. Each tweet article contains: author name (div[data-testid='User-Name']), tweet text (div[data-testid='tweetText']), timestamp (time element), and metric spans. The timeline has three tabs — For You, Following — accessible via nav links. Scroll is required to load more tweets beyond the initial viewport.
reasoning.selector_strategy: Use data-testid='tweet' for tweet article containers — extremely stable across redesigns. Extract sub-fields using relative selectors within each tweet article. Timestamp uses the time element's datetime attribute for machine-readable format.
reasoning.anti_bot_notes: Read-only operation — minimal bot detection risk. Navigate with saved session. Scroll slowly if count > 20 to trigger lazy loading. Do not rapid-fire scroll or extract.
reasoning.performance: Expected run time: 8-15 seconds (navigation 3s + load 2s + extraction 2-8s depending on count). No typing needed.

count.description: Number of tweets to read. Default 20.

filter.description: Timeline filter: 'all' (For You tab), 'following' (Following tab only), 'replies' (include replies in results)

steps.description: Navigate to Twitter/X home timeline and wait for primary column
Verify authenticated — if redirected to login flow, abort with SESSION_EXPIRED
Switch to Following tab if filter is 'following'
Wait for tweet articles to render in timeline
Extract tweet data from timeline articles with scroll-to-load
Return extracted timeline tweets as structured data

error_handling.session_expired: Detect x.com/i/flow/login redirect — abort with SESSION_EXPIRED error
error_handling.empty_timeline: Return empty tweets array with total_extracted: 0 — not an error
error_handling.element_not_found: Log selector tried, capture screenshot, retry once with 15s timeout
error_handling.rate_limited: If rate limit banner detected, wait 60s then retry once
error_handling.following_tab_not_found: Fall back to 'all' filter if Following tab selector not found
